text: Passive treatment modalities produce greater short-term pain reduction compared with eccentric loading protocols for midportion Achilles tendinopathy
Eccentric loading protocols show a nonsignificant trend toward better short-term functional outcomes compared with passive treatments for midportion Achilles tendinopathy
Eccentric loading protocols show a nonsignificant trend toward better mid-term functional outcomes compared with passive treatments for midportion Achilles tendinopathy
No single exercise loading protocol is superior to another for midportion Achilles tendinopathy across short-, mid-, and long-term pain and function outcomes

Clinical practice guidelines recommend eccentric exercises for midportion Achilles tendinopathy, but meta-analytic evidence does not demonstrate their superiority over passive treatments or other loading protocols

Short-term pain relief may be better achieved with passive modalities while eccentric loading may confer greater functional benefits for midportion Achilles tendinopathy, suggesting value in combining both approaches
